title: Delhi Bread Pakora

ingredients.name: bread (white or whole wheat, 8 thick slices)

subs.name: thick-cut sourdough or baguette slices make a sturdier pakora that holds shape better during frying
a squeeze of lemon juice mixed directly into the potato filling
a 3:1 ratio of besan to cornflour gives an exceptionally thin, crisp crust

steps.text: Make the filling: combine the boiled mashed potato with green chilli, coriander leaves, amchur, cumin seeds, red chilli powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix until well combined and taste — the filling should be boldly seasoned with a strong tangy note from the amchur. Divide into 4 equal portions.

Assemble the sandwiches: press a portion of the spiced potato filling onto one bread slice, spreading to the edges. Place a second bread slice on top and press firmly so the sandwich holds together. Trim the crusts if you prefer a neater finish. Repeat with the remaining bread and filling to make 4 filled sandwiches.

Make the besan batter: whisk together besan, rice flour, turmeric, ajwain, a pinch of red chilli powder, and salt in a bowl. Add water gradually, whisking to avoid lumps, until you have a smooth batter thick enough to coat the bread without dripping off immediately — similar in consistency to a pancake batter. Let the batter rest for 2 minutes.

Pour oil into a deep kadai to a depth of 8–10 cm and heat over medium flame to about 170°C — a drop of batter should sizzle and rise to the surface within 2 seconds. Dip each bread sandwich into the batter, coating all four sides and both faces completely. Gently slide into the oil, supporting it as you lower it in. Fry for 3–4 minutes per side until the coating is a deep golden colour and completely set. The oil should bubble steadily throughout.

Drain the bread pakoras on kitchen paper for a minute. Cut each diagonally into two triangles and serve immediately with mint chutney and a generous dollop of Indian pickle on the side. Bread pakoras are best eaten within 10 minutes while the batter coat is crisp and the potato filling inside is still steaming.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: bread → thick-cut sourdough or baguette slices make a sturdier pakora that holds shape better during frying; besan → a 3:1 ratio of besan to cornflour gives an exceptionally thin, crisp crust; amchur → a squeeze of lemon juice mixed directly into the potato filling
